description_long: This sculpture depicts two intertwined figures, one with large, outstretched wings and the other reclining on a draped surface. The winged figure appears to be gently embracing or supporting the other, whose arms are raised toward the face of the winged figure. The composition is dynamic, with the wings creating a dramatic upward sweep and the drapery adding texture and movement. The figures are rendered in a classical style, with detailed anatomy and flowing garments. The base is irregular and textured, suggesting a naturalistic setting. The entire piece is made from a pale, stone-like material, giving it a timeless and monumental appearance.
description: A classical-style sculpture of two intertwined figures, one winged and one reclining, rendered in pale stone with dramatic, sweeping forms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the face-to-face interaction between the winged and reclining figures, with both arms and wings clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the expansive wings and the back of the reclining figure, with the draped base extending outward.
description_view_2: Side-rear view focusing on the winged figure's back and the curvature of the wings, with the reclining figure partially obscured.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the profile of both figures, the flowing drapery, and the intimate gesture between them.
attribute_subject_matter: mythological or allegorical human figures, one with wings
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a neutral, flat surface with no visible environmental context, suggesting an indoor or gallery setting.
